sentenceSubstring: The first such vessel is scheduled to be based in Adak Island
note: Scheduling claim ('is scheduled to be based') for a homeport assignment that never happened; no 4-digit year in the sentence, so the detector cannot reach it. Canonical not-reachable example (rubric Example 4).

sentenceSubstring: Testing of the vehicle is expected to begin in 2020
note: Forward expectation ('is expected to') with a now-past target year (2020); no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. In-lexicon marker + inline past year, so the detector catches it (rubric Example 1).

sentenceSubstring: The bridge was to be completed by the end of 2024
note: Completion claim ('to be completed by the end of 2024') with a now-past year (2024) and no leading dateline. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it. (Borderline: the article revises the date many sentences later, but not in an adjacent sentence, so Exclusion B does not fire.)

sentenceSubstring: Construction in Alaska for the LRDR was scheduled to begin in 2019
note: Forward scheduling claim with a now-past year (2019); no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. The phrase 'was scheduled to' is NOT in the lexicon (only 'is scheduled to'), so the detector misses it.

sentenceSubstring: Testing for Full Operational Capability is expected by 2023
note: Forward expectation with a now-past year (2023); no leading dateline. The phrase 'is expected by' is NOT in the lexicon ('is expected to' requires a following 'to'), so the detector misses it.

sentenceSubstring: The first operation is expected to start from 2025
note: Forward expectation ('is expected to start') with a now-past year (2025) for Japan's AN/SPY-7 first operation; no leading dateline. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it.

sentenceSubstring: The phase one trial is expected to conclude July 2023
note: Forward expectation ('is expected to conclude') with a now-past year (2023) for the NIH mRNA phase-one trial; no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it.

sentenceSubstring: It is scheduled to begin in 2024 with results available in 2028
note: Scheduling claim ('is scheduled to begin') with a now-past start year (2024); no leading dateline. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it (rubric Example 2). The 2028 results date is a future milestone, not the stale claim.

sentenceSubstring: TSMC plans to start volume production of the "3 nm" process node in 2023
note: Forward plan ('plans to start') with a now-past year (2023); no leading dateline. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it.

sentenceSubstring: American manufacturer Intel planned to start "3 nm" production in 2023
note: Forward plan with a now-past year (2023); no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. The phrase 'planned to' is NOT in the lexicon (only 'plans to'), so the detector misses it.

sentenceSubstring: N3P was scheduled to enter volume production in the second half of 2024
note: Forward scheduling claim with a now-past year (2024); no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. The phrase 'was scheduled to' is NOT in the lexicon (only 'is scheduled to'), so the detector misses it.

sentenceSubstring: A logistics center in Everett, Washington, is expected to open in June 2024
note: Forward expectation ('is expected to open') with a now-past year (2024) for the Everett logistics center; no leading dateline, not resolved nearby. In-lexicon marker + inline year; the detector catches it.
